title: Portfolio MRI decision-support summary
subtitle: Sample portfolio review for stakeholder discussion

sections.title: Current decision view
sections.body: The current portfolio is understandable, but its main risk story is concentrated US equity and growth-factor exposure. Bonds, gold, and cash provide some ballast, but do not remove the concentration issue. Current evidence supports a no-trade stance for now rather than an immediate material rebalance.

sections.title: Evidence
sections.body: The X-Ray and Stress evidence is suitable for a sample review discussion. It should not be treated as conclusive advice or as a substitute for client-specific suitability review.

sections.title: Candidate hypothesis
sections.body: Equal Weight was tested as a diagnostic benchmark. It is not a recommendation. It helps show what changes when concentration is mechanically reduced.

sections.title: Comparison
sections.body: The candidate lowers the largest holding and equity concentration, but it also changes the intended exposure mix and increases cash/gold weight. The trade-off is not one-sided.

sections.title: Verdict boundary
sections.body: The current verdict supports no-trade for now. This means the evidence does not justify a material rebalance in this sample review, not that the current portfolio is perfect.

monitoring: Monitor concentration, evidence quality, and mandate changes before reopening the decision.
boundaryNote: This report explains decision-support evidence. It is not advice, suitability review, or a trading instruction.
